Your plan was attacked on four lenses — including BAR-LOWERING, which caught every previous plan.

[
 {
  "reasoning": "REFUTED under the BAR-LOWERING lens. The plan does not aim at beating 0.5578; it aims at LANDING ON it, installs a lower fallback threshold, and pre-writes the excuse for missing.

THE ARITHMETIC IS THE CATCH — every dimension target is AT OR BELOW cellcog's published dimension score.
cellcog (leaderboard_gpt55.csv, verified): comprehensiveness .5634 / insight .5708 / instruction .5530 / readability .5194.
The plan's EXPECTED block:
- INSIGHT -> ~0.555  vs cellcog .5708  = BELOW
- COMPREHENSIVENESS -> ~0.555  vs cellcog .5634  = BELOW
- INSTRUCTION -> ~0.552  vs cellcog .5530  = BELOW
- READABILITY -> ~0.520  vs cellcog .5194  = parity, explicitly capped by fiat
You cannot exceed a weighted average while sitting at or under it on all four components. The plan's own numbers therefore make beating cellcog IMPOSSIBLE BY CONSTRUCTION; the 0.557 total only "reaches" 0.5578 by rounding. It never nominates a single dimension where it intends to EXCEED cellcog's measured dimension score.

The contradiction is quotable and internal: the plan calls comprehensiveness "our genuine out-score lane" ("120-150 on-topic journal works vs cellcog ~100... evidence volume is the lane where we out-score rather than match") and then sets that lane's target at 0.555 — BELOW cellcog's 0.5634. It plans 1.3-1.5x the evidence and budgets a LOWER score than the system it is out-evidencing. That is the bar being lowered inside the very lane the plan declares its edge.

WHERE IT SETTLES — verbatim:
1. "VERDICT: this LANDS ON 0.5578, it does not safely BEAT it."
2. "We therefore run two thresholds: beat-best-measured (0.5441+margin, decidable) and beat-estimate (0.5578+margin, the stretch)." — This is bodhi reinstalled as an acceptable success criterion. The brief's first line: "TARGET: > 0.5578 (cellcog-max, #1). NOT parity. NOT bodhi. THE TOP." The plan re-admits the exact fallback the brief banned, and labels the real target "the stretch."
3. "first full pass ~0.545-0.557; beat-with-margin requires 2-4 wheel iterations and is plausible, not guaranteed." — the first pass's own midpoint (0.551) is below the target.
4. "the honest report is: we beat every measured task-72 score on the board, and whether we beat cellcog is unmeasurable at judge noise" — a PRE-WRITTEN VICTORY STATEMENT for a run that misses the target. The plan has already drafted what it will say when it fails.

THE SOFT-TARGET MOVE IS THE BAR-LOWERING MECHANISM. The CEILING section re-derives cellcog's task-72 as "~0.556 +/- 0.019, possibly below bodhi's measured 0.5441." The unit correction is factually right (cellcog ships only race_result.txt, no per-task rows — I verified). But the plan uses that correct fact to CONVERT the goal from a fixed number into a distribution wide enough that a 0.545-0.557 outcome can be declared a win. Correct diagnosis, weaponized as an alibi. Note the asymmetry: the same regression says cellcog's task-72 could be ~0.575 — and the plan budgets nothing for that branch.

READABILITY CAPPED BY FIAT. "READABILITY IS CAPPED: nobody on the board exceeds ~0.52... budget no gains above it." A ceiling inferred from 10 observations of systems that all write the same way, applied to the one dimension the plan itself calls "fully mechanical" (100w paragraphs, H3/H4, no walls). If the fix is mechanical and the field is undifferentiated (all 10 systems sit 0.457-0.532), that is the cheapest place to actually EXCEED #1 — and the plan pre-emptively forbids trying. Cost of assuming the cap and being wrong: ~0 (we do the same work). Cost of assuming it and being right: nothing lost. So the cap has no decision value; it exists only to shrink the expected total to a number the plan can defend.

WHAT A NON-BAR-LOWERING PLAN WOULD LOOK LIKE. Same machinery, retargeted: every dimension aimed ABOVE cellcog's published number (insight >= .58 not .555; comprehensiveness >= .58 not .555; instruction >= .57; readability attacked to .54+ rather than conceded at .52), one threshold only (0.5578 + margin), and no fallback narrative. The mechanisms in RETRIEVAL/SYNTHESIS/COMPOSER are mostly sound and mostly aimed at COPYING cellcog (copy the insight engine, copy the monograph architecture, copy the citation template, copy the era spine). A plan built almost entirely from "copy #1" has a natural ceiling of #1 — the only additive lever is evidence volume, and the plan then under-budgets that lever below #1's own score. That is why the arithmetic comes out at a tie: the design IS a tie.

Default was refuted=true if uncertain; here there is no uncertainty. The plan states the retreat itself.",
  "refuted": true,
  "fix": "Three changes, none of which touch the retrieval/synthesis/composer machinery (which is fine) — only the target-setting, which is where the retreat lives:

1. DELETE THE SECOND THRESHOLD. Strike "beat-best-measured (0.5441+margin)" and the sentence "we beat every measured task-72 score on the board, and whether we beat cellcog is unmeasurable at judge noise." One threshold: mean >= 0.5578 + margin. A plan that ships with a fallback success criterion will hit the fallback.

2. RETARGET EVERY DIMENSION ABOVE cellcog's PUBLISHED SCORE, and make each an owned lever:
   - comprehensiveness .4549 -> >= 0.58 (cellcog .5634). This is the declared out-score lane; budget it as one. 150+ on-topic journal works, >=35% 2024+, methods texture (N/design/identification) on every cited empirical claim — cellcog cannot match this without our corpus.
   - insight .4238 -> >= 0.58 (cellcog .5708). Do not just replicate cellcog's 8 named syntheses; ship 10-12 AND ground a subset in the contradiction index (a synthesis that ADJUDICATES two cited findings is strictly stronger to a judge than one that only declares a gap). That is the one insight move cellcog structurally cannot make: it has no verified card corpus to adjudicate from.
   - instruction .4409 -> >= 0.57 (cellcog .5530).
   - readability .3774 -> attack to >= 0.54, do NOT concede at 0.52. The plan itself says the fix is mechanical; the 0.52 "cap" is 10 samples of systems that all write in walls. Cost of trying is zero.
   Target total then reads ~0.575, with the tie at 0.5578 as the DOWNSIDE case, not the plan.

3. NAME THE ASYMMETRIC BRANCH. The same regression that says cellcog's task-72 might be 0.556 says it might be 0.575. Plan for 0.575. If the true value is lower, we win by more; if the plan is built for 0.5578 and the truth is 0.575, we lose and call it noise — which is precisely the outcome the current CEILING section is pre-authorizing."
 },
 {
  "refuted": true,
  "reasoning": "REFUTED through the FABRICATION lens. Six concrete paths by which a FALSE claim reaches the page; one is executable against the repo's own adversarial suite, one is MANDATORY on every insight sentence, one sits on the mainline of every paragraph.

(1) EXECUTABLE PROOF — the plan's own named edit breaks the repo's own fabrication test. synthesis_contract.py gate #6 is sound for exactly one reason: the mechanism pool is the premises OF THE SENTENCE. The plan directs "fix synthesis_contract.py:197-205 so the mechanism check runs against the declared mechanisms of ANY cited card in the corpus, not only the two premises being compared." I emulated it. Sentence "...a divergence that reflects adoption lag across firms." citing only p1,p2:
  TODAY (premise-scoped): (False, 'causal_language_with_no_stated_mechanism:"reflects"')
  UNDER THE PLAN (corpus-wide): (True, '')
That is verbatim the repo's shipped ATTACK #1 ('imports a mechanism (adoption lag) no premise states'). The plan flips a fabrication regression test to PASS — and for no gain: bodhi's composite bridge CITES paper B, so B is already a premise and current code already admits it. The edit overshoots its own stated purpose by exactly the unsafe delta: it admits mechanisms from cards the sentence does NOT cite, producing a causal claim narratively attributed to A and B, neither of whom stated it — a fabricated attribution, produced deterministically. And the check is a substring test (any(mech in s.lower())); with 120-150 cards the pool becomes the union of every mechanism in the field ("automation","adoption","displacement","monitoring"), so gate #6 degenerates to "does the sentence contain a word the field uses." Not weakened — disabled.

(2) THE NEUTRAL ROUTER CONVERTS THE HALLUCINATION DETECTOR INTO A FORMATTER. entailment_judge.py:588 defines NEUTRAL as "the SENTENCE introduces a fact, entity, mechanism, or specificity NOT present in the SPAN" — that verdict IS the hallucination signal. The plan: "NEUTRAL sentences route to contract classes 2-4 instead of deletion." Trace: composer emits "Algorithmic management in warehouse logistics may raise injury rates by compressing task cycle times below recovery thresholds," citing a surveillance card that says nothing of the kind. Judge -> NEUTRAL. Today: deleted. Under the plan: routed to class 4, which requires only (i) hedge verb, (ii) [Analytical Hypothesis] tag, (iii) gap declaration — all COSTUME, not proof. Class 4 has no premise list, so gates #4 (no new number) and #5 (no new entity, checked against the premise blob) have nothing to check against and cannot run at all. Specification gap vs the model being copied: cellcog's real class-4 sentences are CONDITIONAL EXTRAPOLATIONS OVER CITED FINDINGS (verified in cellcog-max.jsonl: "If generative AI disproportionately exposes female-dominated work *and* women adopt the technology less, the gender wage gap could..." — built on Dorta-Gonzalez 2024, cited). The plan's rule licenses ANY un-sourced mechanism wearing three tags. It copies cellcog's costume, not cellcog's constraint.

(3) GAP DECLARATIONS ARE FALSE CLAIMS MANUFACTURED ON A SCHEDULE. Class-4 condition (iii) MANDATES "No peer-reviewed study has yet measured X," verified against a gap index derived from a 120-150-item snowball whose stopping rule is local saturation (<2 new works per 20 queries), not literature coverage (10^4+ papers exist). That is a universal negative existential over the world's literature asserted from a convenience sample. It is FALSE whenever such a study sits outside the corpus, and the error is systematically correlated — you make the gap claim precisely in the thin cells where retrieval is weakest. Mandated 6-8+ times per report and STRUCTURALLY UNCHECKABLE: no span gate can verify the absence of spans. The plan names this as a feature ("gaps get narrated as findings ... which no faithfulness gate can cut") — a written admission of an unguarded channel. It also violates the plan's own STILL-DELETED list ("universals") and would require weakening the repo's UNIVERSAL regex to ship.

(4) MANDATORY METHODS TEXTURE OVER OPTIONAL SOURCE FIELDS IS A HALLUCINATION PUMP. The floor is "any number not in a source span is deleted," but the span is an LLM-consolidated EVIDENCE CARD (retrieval step (h)); the plan never requires cards to be verbatim-extractive with offsets. LLM writes card -> card becomes trusted span -> composer cites card -> number gate passes. Meanwhile the composer mandates N/design/identification "on cited empirical claims ... from card fields," while Crossref abstracts routinely omit N and identification (the plan itself permits metadata-only cards). A required output field with an absent input is the canonical condition under which a model invents. Result: a fabricated sample size welded to a real author, real journal, real DOI.

(5) NARRATIVE CITATIONS UPGRADE EVERY BINDING ERROR INTO A FABRICATED ATTRIBUTION. Deleting [n] and moving attribution into prose means an off-by-one card binding is no longer a wrong number but a false factual claim about a named person and journal. The entailment judge compares SENTENCE to SPAN; the author name lives in the sentence, not the span, so nothing checks prose name/venue against the card. Venue comes from Crossref container-title, which returns "SSRN Electronic Journal"/"NBER Working Paper Series"/preprint mirrors for a nontrivial share of records — so both the working-paper quarantine AND the in-prose journal name ride on an unreliable field.

(6) CLASS-3 CERTIFIES CAUSATION WITH A METADATA DIFF. Two cards differing on `level` proves they differ on level; it does NOT prove the level difference EXPLAINS their divergent findings (period, country, coding error). The contract stamps an uncited causal explanation as verified. The plan's own exemplar ("The parallel is not a contradiction but a demonstration...") contains no hedge, violating the plan's own "uncited, HEDGED resolution" rule in the sentence it chose to showcase.

The plan asserts in prose that "fabricated facts/numbers/attributions remain impossible" while the three code changes it names remove the scoping that makes that true. The danger is not that it takes a measured risk — it is that it believes the floor still holds.

Files: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py (gates 4-7 + ATTACKS suite), /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py:588, /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cleaner_survival_test.py, /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/cellcog-max.jsonl.",
  "fix": "Keep the strategy (insight engine, monograph architecture, journal-first retrieval) but restore scoping to the three places the plan breaks it:

1. DO NOT widen the mechanism pool to the corpus. Widen it to the UNION OF THE SENTENCE'S OWN CITED CARDS. That is all bodhi's composite bridge needs (if the mechanism comes from paper B and B is cited, B is a premise). Add a compatibility guard: a mechanism may cross from card B to a finding from card A only if B and A share at least one declared dimension (era / sector / level) — blocks transplanting a 1980s-computerisation mechanism onto Kenyan gig work. Re-run scripts/synthesis_contract.py --self-test and require ATTACK #1 to still REJECT; make that a CI gate.

2. CLASS 4 MUST CARRY PREMISES. Change the rule from "un-sourced mechanism legal if hedged+tagged+gap-declared" to "an ANALYTICAL SYNTHESIS is legal iff it is a CONDITIONAL over >=2 admitted premises (cellcog's actual form: 'If [premise A] and [premise B], then X could...'), AND hedged, AND tagged." Then it still passes validate() with premise_ids, so gates #4 (no new number) and #5 (no new entity) still run. This is what cellcog actually does and it costs nothing on the insight dimension.

3. NEUTRAL IS NOT A ROUTER. A NEUTRAL verdict may only trigger a REWRITE-AND-RE-JUDGE loop (rewrite the sentence to be entailed by its span, or re-route to a class that supplies premises and re-validate). A sentence that cannot pass any class with premises is DELETED, as today. Never allow "add a hedge + a tag" to convert a NEUTRAL into a publication.

4. GAP CLAIMS MUST BE CORPUS-SCOPED IN PROSE, ALWAYS. Ban the bare "No peer-reviewed study has measured X." Mandate the scoped form: "Within the 137 journal articles retrieved for this review, none measures X" or cellcog's hedge "questions the peer-reviewed literature has not yet answered." This preserves the entire insight/instruction payoff (the judge still sees gap-narration and epistemic honesty) while making the claim TRUE by construction. Add a deterministic regex gate rejecting unscoped negative existentials.

5. EVIDENCE CARDS MUST BE EXTRACTIVE. Every card field usable as a span (N, design, identification, finding, mechanism) must be a verbatim quote with a character offset into the fetched abstract/full text, or be NULL. Methods texture is emitted ONLY from non-null fields; missing texture is narrated as missing ("the abstract does not report the sample size"), never filled. No LLM-written field may ever serve as the ground-truth span for the number gate.

6. ATTRIBUTION BINDING CHECK. Add a deterministic gate: every author surname and journal name appearing in prose must string-match the `authors`/`container-title` of a card the sentence is entailed against. Reject on mismatch. Cross-check container-title against a journal allowlist (ISSN) so SSRN/NBER records can never be rendered in prose as journals.

7. CLASS 3 MUST HEDGE AND MUST NOT CLAIM CAUSATION. Allowed: "these estimates observe different units of analysis" (a fact about the cards). Banned: "the divergence IS EXPLAINED BY the level difference" (a causal claim a metadata diff cannot support). Fix the plan's own exemplar accordingly."
 },
 {
  "refuted": true,
  "reasoning": "REFUTED on the judge-invisibility lens by direct measurement against the production RACE cleaner.

I ran the production ArticleCleaner (/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/third_party/deep_research_bench/prompt/clean_prompt.py) twice over a 988-word chunk of cellcog-max id=72 (/home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/cellcog-max.jsonl) containing the legend paragraph, the tagged syntheses, and 14 narrative citations.

MEASURED (raw -> cleaned run0 / run1):
- epistemic tags (*[Established Finding]* etc.): 7 -> 7 / 7  (SURVIVE)
- H2/H3 headings: 5 -> 5 / 5  (SURVIVE)
- parenthetical years "(2019)": 14 -> 0 / 0  (DESTROYED, 100%)

Corroborated by the project's existing /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/cleaner_survival.json (12 facts x 5 formats):
- C_narrative_author_year "Acemoglu and Restrepo (2019) show...": authors 5.0/12, years 0.0/12
- D_narrative_with_journal "Writing in the JEP, Acemoglu and Restrepo (2019) show...": authors 10/12, venues 12/12, years 0.0/12
- E_journal_prefix "In their 2019 JEP article, Acemoglu and Restrepo show...": authors 10/12, venues 9/12, years 10/12  <- the ONLY format where the year reaches the judge

THE REFUTATION. COMPOSER item (1) explicitly mandates "YEAR STAYS IN PARENS (160/177 of cellcog's year-parens are this narrative form and it wins; the brief's 'year MUST be prose' rule is refuted)." That 160/177 count was taken from cellcog's PRE-CLEANER submission. The judge never saw one of those year-parens: the cleaner deletes 100% of them, verified on cellcog's own sentences. The plan therefore commits precisely the category error it convicts the brief of in DIAGNOSIS (1) -- inferring judge-visible strength from an artifact the judge does not read -- and uses that non-evidence to overturn the one rule the project's only measurement actually supports, dismissing format E (the strictly dominant format on every axis) as a "contortion."

WHY IT COSTS SCORE, NOT JUST TIDINESS. Recency is a named pillar of the plan's two biggest bets: COMPREHENSIVENESS +0.029 ("recency through 2026", ">=35% dated 2024+", retrieval recency bonus, "newer sources ... cellcog cannot exceed") and the stated beat-with-margin condition (converting the comprehensiveness edge into +0.01). Under the mandated template the judge sees ZERO years anywhere in the body -- a 2026 meta-analysis and a 1974 paper are indistinguishable to it. The entire retrieval-side recency weighting is real work that terminates before the judge. The lever is invisible, so it cannot move a criterion into the top band.

SECOND LENS HIT (same root). "120-150 works vs cellcog's ~100" converts to judge-visible signal only via prose attributions, since [n] markers and the reference list are deleted (the plan concedes this). The plan sets a CORPUS target but no PROSE-ATTRIBUTION target. Cellcog gets ~73 narrated works in front of the judge inside 16,334 words (measured; the plan states 13,486 -- also wrong), while the plan caps the body at 12,500-14,000 words. A bigger corpus rendered at lower prose-attribution density reads to the judge as parity-or-worse on the exact dimension the plan chose as its out-score lane.

WHAT THE LENS CONFIRMS AS SAFE (so this is a targeted refutation, not a wholesale one): the 0.32-weight insight engine survives -- square-bracket epistemic tags are NOT eaten as "citation marks" (7/7, both runs) -- and headings survive, so the readability levers (H3/H4, short paragraphs) do reach the judge. Author names and journal names in prose survive at 10/12 and 12/12. The plan's core architecture is sound; its citation template and its evidence-volume-to-judge conversion are not.",
  "fix": "1) Replace the COMPOSER citation template with the measured-dominant form E (year and journal in PROSE, author as grammatical subject): "In their 2024 *Journal of Economic Perspectives* article, Acemoglu and Restrepo show that ..." -- measured authors 10/12, years 10/12, venues 9/12 post-cleaner, versus 0/12 years for every parenthetical variant. Delete the "YEAR STAYS IN PARENS" rule and the 160/177 justification (it counts features of an un-cleaned artifact).
2) Add a hard composer target on judge-visible attribution, not corpus size: >=110 distinct in-prose author+journal attributions, of which >=35% carry a prose year in 2024-2026. Corpus count (120-150) is an input; prose-attribution count is the only thing the judge can score.
3) Raise the body target to ~15,000-16,000 words to match cellcog's real length (measured 16,334, not the 13,486 stated in the plan), otherwise the plan fights the #1 with less ammunition on its own architecture.
4) Keep the epistemic tags exactly as specified -- verified to survive the cleaner 7/7 -- and keep the H2/H3/H4 plan; those levers are confirmed judge-visible.
5) Before trusting any per-dimension estimate, re-run /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cleaner_survival_test.py against the ACTUAL composed draft (not synthetic sentences) and assert post-cleaner: 0 lost tags, >=100 surviving author names, >=35 surviving 2024+ years, all headings intact. Make that a gate, not a spot check."
 },
 {
  "refuted": true,
  "reasoning": "REFUTED on the retrieval-reality lens — the count is reachable, but "~100-150 canonical journal articles WITH usable text, >=35% dated 2024+, methods texture on every cited claim" is a wish, and the plan's decisive stage has no working implementation as written.

(1) BOTH FORWARD-CITATION CHANNELS THE PLAN NAMES ARE DEAD ON THIS BOX (verified live, today). Plan stage (c) — "the decisive stage keyword search provably cannot do" — is "cited-by via Crossref/OpenAlex". Crossref's free API has NO cited-by list (only is-referenced-by-count). OpenAlex LIST endpoints (?search=, ?filter=cites:) return HTTP 429 on every attempt with 5x exponential backoff; only single-entity GETs (/works/doi:...) succeed (20/20 at 3/s). So the plan's stated expansion mechanism cannot run at all. It IS rescuable — OpenCitations COCI works and I confirmed it (1334 citing DOIs for Acemoglu-Restrepo 2019, 2141 for Autor 2015, 296 for Eloundou 2024) — but COCI is nowhere in the plan, and its coverage is Crossref-open-references-only (the Frey-Osborne query failed mid-stream with IncompleteRead). The plan as written is not executable; a repaired plan is.

(2) THE RECENCY LANE IS THE TEXT-POOR LANE. Live funnel: 90 random COCI citing-DOIs of A-R 2019 through journal_corpus_build.py's own gates -> 26 accepted (29%), 19 of them 2024+. Of those 19: 5 OA (4 with PDF), 9 with ANY retrievable abstract (Crossref ∪ OpenAlex single-entity), and 10 with NO retrievable text whatsoever. Every Elsevier venue in the on-topic recent set (Technological Forecasting, Technology in Society, Energy, China Economic Review, Finance Research Letters, Economic Analysis and Policy, J. Asian Economics) deposits no abstract to Crossref and has none in OpenAlex. Usable-text rate on the 2024+ lane ~= 47%, and the abstracts that do exist are ~98-250 words — they almost never state N, design, or identification strategy. Plan item COMPOSER-(12) "METHODS TEXTURE mandatory on cited empirical claims: N, design, identification, in prose from card fields" therefore cannot be satisfied from the card fields that will actually exist; it will either fire a deletion cascade or be silently relaxed.

(3) THE FETCH PIPELINE IS WEAKER TODAY THAN WHEN 36/70 WAS MEASURED. /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/journal_corpus_content.json: 70 works, 34 CITATION_ONLY / 24 ABSTRACT_ONLY / 12 FULLTEXT (36/70 groundable, exactly the "36/70" in the lens). Two of deep_fetch.py's three recovery paths are Semantic Scholar (semantic_scholar_pdf, deep_fetch.py:78; s2_abstract, deep_fetch.py:86) — S2 now returns 404/429 for this box. So a rebuild today degrades to Unpaywall+Crossref+OpenAlex-entity only. Scaling the measured rates: a 120-150-work corpus yields roughly 55-75 GROUNDABLE cards and ~15-25 with full text. That is not 2x cellcog's evidence; under our own no-fabrication contract, metadata-only cards can be NAMED but never used to state what a paper found — while cellcog (71% of sentences uncited) states findings for all ~100 of its refs from model knowledge. The claimed "genuine out-score lane" (COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.4549 -> 0.555, +0.029 weighted) rests on groundable-source superiority that the measurement says will be parity at best.

(4) THE EXISTING CORPUS PROVES THE STRUCTURAL POINT: journal_corpus.json is 70 works with year max 2023 and ZERO from 2024+, because backward reference expansion cannot reach papers newer than its anchors. The ">=35% dated 2024+" requirement is 100% dependent on the forward channel in (1).

(5) RECENCY AND "HIGH-QUALITY" ARE IN DIRECT CONFLICT IN THE ONLY CHANNEL THAT WORKS. The reachable 2024+ set is not canonical: sampled accepted venues include Energy, Finance Research Letters, Emerging Markets Finance and Trade, Environment Development and Sustainability, Int. J. of System Assurance Engineering, J. of Chinese Economic and Business Studies, Scientific Reports — overwhelmingly China firm-panel robot econometrics with near-zero citations. Forcing 35%+ from 2024+ mechanically drags the corpus into a low-prestige tail on a task whose instruction is literally "only cites high-quality... journal articles," putting at risk the very INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING dimension the plan books +0.028 on. The quality weight in step (f) cannot manufacture canonical 2024+ works that do not exist in the reachable set.

(6) THE COVERAGE MATRIX CANNOT BE FILLED. The anchors are five economics-of-automation papers; forward citation from them cannot populate cells for algorithmic management/surveillance, gender, Global South informality, education, or healthcare. The only channel for those cells is keyword search — which journal_corpus_build.py's own header documents as returning ResNet, SMOTE, and near-predatory low-citation venues. The 9x8x3x5 (~1000-cell) matrix will terminate almost entirely through the "declared literature gap" escape hatch, so "stopped by a coverage matrix, not a count" degrades to "a count plus a large pile of gap declarations."

BOTTOM LINE: the 120-150 count is achievable (one anchor alone yields 1334 candidates at 29% gate-pass), but "canonical" and "with usable text" are not. Realistic ceiling on this box: ~120-150 named works of which ~55-75 are groundable, ~20 full-text, 2024+ share achievable but composed of mid-tier, uncited, abstract-less papers. Retrieval is not the out-score lane the EXPECTED section says it is; it is the plan's weakest link.",
  "fix": "Repairs that make the retrieval layer honest and executable:
1. REPLACE the forward-citation channel: drop "cited-by via Crossref/OpenAlex" (both dead) and wire OpenCitations COCI (https://opencitations.net/index/coci/api/v1/citations/{doi}) into /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/journal_corpus_build.py as the forward edge, keeping Crossref /works/{doi} references as the backward edge. Handle IncompleteRead with streaming retries (Frey-Osborne fails on first read).
2. KEEP OpenAlex, but only as a SINGLE-ENTITY lookup (/works/doi:{doi}) for abstract_inverted_index — it works at 3/s and recovers ~4-5 abstracts per 19 recent works that Crossref lacks. Never call OpenAlex list/filter/search endpoints; they 429 unconditionally.
3. DELETE the Semantic Scholar fallbacks in scripts/deep_fetch.py:78-88 (404/429) or gate them behind an S2 API key; today they silently return nothing and inflate the perceived fetch budget.
4. REPLACE the ">=35% dated 2024+" hard rule with ">=35% dated 2024+ AMONG GROUNDABLE (abstract-or-fulltext) CARDS," and set the honest target at 120-150 NAMED works / 55-75 GROUNDABLE works, not 120-150 groundable. Report both numbers in the corpus audit so no downstream stage assumes text exists.
5. DOWNGRADE COMPOSER item (12): methods texture becomes "mandatory WHEN the card carries an abstract stating N/design/identification, else the claim is cited by title-and-venue only." Otherwise the synthesis contract will delete most 2024+ claims.
6. ADD a venue-standing floor to the recency bin (e.g. require the 2024+ works to clear a citation-percentile or venue-tier gate, or cap the low-tier China-panel share) so that satisfying recency does not violate the task's "high-quality journal articles" instruction.
7. RE-BASELINE the EXPECTED comprehensiveness gain: with groundable-source parity rather than 1.5x superiority, the +0.029 weighted on COMPREHENSIVENESS is not supported by the retrieval measurements; the honest first-pass band drops toward the low end (0.535-0.550) unless a working full-text channel (institutional access, publisher TDM, or paid S2/OpenAlex key) is added.
8. STOP the coverage matrix from lying: for cells only reachable by keyword search (gender, surveillance/algorithmic management, Global South informality, education, healthcare), declare them keyword-only up front and measure the junk rate before counting on them."
 }
]

Write the FINAL SINGLE PLAN to BEAT 0.5578. Rebut or fix every refutation with evidence. Do NOT retreat.
If it honestly lands below 0.5578, say exactly where it falls short and what would close it — but do not silently
re-target. And state plainly: what assumption is this plan STILL making that we have not tested?

Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.

# POLARIS BENCHMARK TARGET — the real scoreboard (from web search 2026-07-11)

## What we must beat
**FS-Researcher** (arXiv:2602.01566, ACL 2026) — "Test-Time Scaling for Long-Horizon Research
Tasks with File-System-Based Agents". A file-system dual-agent (Context Builder + Report Writer)
that scales research BEYOND the context window via a persistent knowledge base. This is the same
thesis as POLARIS (process ALL 329 baskets (current s2s3 iter-7 count; 346 was the stale iter-6 count), not one context window). It claims **SOTA report
quality** across backbones. Code: github.com/Ignoramus0817/FS-Researcher.

## The benchmarks it is scored on (both open-ended report quality)
1. **DeepResearch Bench** (arXiv:2506.11763) — 100 PhD-level research tasks.
   - Scorer: **RACE** — reference-based LLM judge, 4 dimensions with per-task DYNAMIC weights:
     Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability.
     Compares the target report against a high-quality REFERENCE report.
   - Download: `github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench` — tasks at
     `data/prompt_data/query.jsonl` (100 tasks); HF dataset `muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Dataset`.
   - Leaderboard (ChatGPT/Gemini deep-research RACE scores) is on HuggingFace.
2. **DeepConsult** — open-ended consulting-style report quality (pairwise/quality judge).
3. (also referenced for the agent side: BrowseComp, BFCL, TAU-Bench, MCP Agent Bench.)

Related: **DeepResearch Bench II** (arXiv:2601.08536) — rubric-from-expert-report diagnosis
(the DRB-II wrapper already stubbed in the compete wheel).

## What this means for POLARIS
- **RACE is the correct instrument** — confirms the compete wheel's direction; DeepTRACE (faithfulness)
  is a SEPARATE, complementary axis, not the mission's quality axis.
- "Beat FS-Researcher" is now RUNNABLE: run POLARIS on the DeepResearch Bench 100 tasks, score with
  RACE against the reference reports, compare to FS-Researcher + the ChatGPT/Gemini leaderboard.
- The two build wheels serve this end:
  - **OUTLINE** → depth + compute-and-prove drives Comprehensiveness + Insight (RACE dims 1-2) and
    the faithfulness moat (DeepTRACE) that frontier products lack.
  - **COMPOSE** → renders all 329 baskets (current s2s3 iter-7 count; 346 was the stale iter-6 count) fast + faithful so nothing is dropped for latency =
    Comprehensiveness + Readability, at full corpus depth.
- Open gap: we still have NO FS-Researcher report artifact locally; the benchmark download closes it
  (we can run POLARIS on the same 100 tasks and score head-to-head).

TASK 72 PROMPT:
Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is causing significant disruptions and affecting various industries. Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles.

DIMENSION WEIGHTS: {"readability": 0.14, "insight": 0.32, "comprehensiveness": 0.29, "instruction_following": 0.25}

=== COMPREHENSIVENESS (weight 0.29) ===
- [w=0.10] Grounding in AI and the Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) Context :: Assesses whether the review adequately defines AI within the context of the 4IR and explains its fundamental role as a driver of labor market restructuring. This contextual framing is crucial for understanding the scope and nature of the impacts discussed.
- [w=0.25] Breadth of Labor Market Restructuring Dimensions Covered :: Evaluates the extent to which the review addresses the multifaceted nature of AI's impact on the labor market, including job creation, job displacement, job transformation, changes in skill demands, wage effects, and productivity changes.
- [w=0.25] Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis :: Checks if the review examines the restructuring impact of AI across a diverse range of relevant industries, highlighting both common trends and sector-specific nuances. The task explicitly requires focusing on 'various industries.'
- [w=0.15] Exploration of AI's Disruptive Character and Scale :: Assesses whether the review sufficiently explores the 'significant disruptions' caused by AI, including the magnitude, speed, and transformative potential of these changes on labor market structures and dynamics.

This ensures the review's content is built upon a solid and comprehensive foundation of existing knowledge.
- [w=0.10] Balanced Discussion of AI's Labor Market Impacts :: Assesses whether the review presents a nuanced perspective by discussing both the challenges (e.g., displacement, skill gaps, inequality) and opportunities (e.g., new job creation, productivity gains, enhanced work quality) arising from AI's influence on the labor market.

class _RetryableJudgeError(Exception):
    """I-transport-001 (#1191) Site 4: a transient/recoverable judge fault that should trigger a
    bounded SAME-provider retry. Carries the human-readable `reason` used for the terminal
    ('ENTAILED','judge_error: <reason>') sentinel so a bad-verdict's `bad_verdict=<v>` detail is
    preserved across retries instead of being collapsed into a generic exception type name."""

    def __init__(self, reason: str) -> None:
        super().__init__(reason)
        self.reason = reason
_ENTAILMENT_PROMPT = """You are a strict entailment judge. You will be given a SPAN of source text and a SENTENCE that cites that span. Decide whether the SPAN entails the SENTENCE.

Rules:
- ENTAILED: every factual assertion in the SENTENCE is supported by the SPAN. Conservative paraphrase is allowed.
- NEUTRAL: the SENTENCE introduces a fact, entity, mechanism, or specificity NOT present in the SPAN (e.g. SPAN says "GLP-1 RAs", SENTENCE says "semaglutide"; SPAN says "adipocyte metabolism", SENTENCE adds "lipid metabolism" or "energy storage"; SPAN has numbers but not the specific claim being made).
- CONTRADICTED: the SENTENCE asserts something the SPAN explicitly disagrees with.

Return STRICT JSON only, no prose:
{{"verdict": "ENTAILED" | "NEUTRAL" | "CONTRADICTED", "reason": "<one short sentence>"}}

SPAN:
{span}

SENTENCE:
{sentence}

JSON:"""

input.the_wheel: FIX -> COMPOSE -> GATE -> SCORE k=5 -> READ -> FIX. One lever per iteration, always paired against the reigning champion draft. (1) FIX: exactly one dimension-targeted change, motivated by the previous READ step. (2) COMPOSE the full report through the contract lane. (3) INTEGRITY GATES before any scoring money is spent — all must be green: synthesis_contract.py --self-test with ATTACK #1 REJECTED; cleaner_survival_test.py against the ACTUAL draft asserting 0 lost epistemic tags, >=100 surviving author names, >=35 surviving 2024+ prose years, all headings intact; unscoped-negative-existential regex = 0 hits; attribution binding gate = 0 mismatches; a red gate blocks the score run, full stop. (4) SCORE k=5 with the GPT-5.5 judge (judge SD 0.0074; smallest resolvable paired effect +0.0094 — never n=1). (5) READ the CLEANED prose line by line against the 25 rubric criteria of race_task72_criteria.txt, mapping every weak band to a criterion (the qualitative gate the operator demanded); also read the outline decision trace (gap queries fired, demotions, routing) per the standing monitor. (6) Keep the lever only if paired delta >= +0.0094; a lever that fails once is REVERTED, not tweaked. Iteration order (largest headroom first, from the EXPECTED arithmetic): readability mechanics -> attribution density/form E -> insight syntheses+adjudications -> 4IR spine and industry matrix -> corpus expansion rounds via COCI. STOPPING CONDITION (the only one): mean k=5 >= 0.5578 + 0.0094 with all integrity gates green. There is no secondary stopping condition and no fallback success state.
input.expected_total: DESIGN TARGETS — every dimension ABOVE cellcog's published score (leaderboard_gpt55.csv), each an owned lever, weights from race_task72_criteria.txt: COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.4549 -> 0.58 (cellcog .5634; lever: 120-150 named works with >=110 judge-visible prose attributions vs cellcog's ~73 narrated, full industry-x-dimension matrix, 15-16k words); INSIGHT 0.4238 -> 0.58 (cellcog .5708; lever: 10-12 named syntheses of which >=4 adjudicate contradictions from the card corpus — the move cellcog structurally cannot make — plus mechanisms criterion, 4IR-integration criterion, future-agenda criterion each explicitly serviced); INSTRUCTION 0.4409 -> 0.57 (cellcog .5530; lever: journal-only ISSN-verified citations narrated in a Scope-and-Methods section, consistent focus, disruptions and various-industries criteria explicitly addressed); READABILITY 0.3774 -> 0.54 attacked, not conceded (cellcog .5194; lever: fully mechanical — 100w paragraphs, 30+ H3, structured abstract, summary tables, transitions). TOTAL: 0.29x0.58 + 0.32x0.58 + 0.25x0.57 + 0.14x0.54 = 0.5719 ~= 0.572. YES — it BEATS 0.5578, by +0.014, which exceeds the +0.0094 smallest resolvable paired effect at k=5. Sanity anchor from 0.4382: the weighted deltas are +0.036 (compreh) +0.050 (insight) +0.032 (instr) +0.023 (read) = +0.141, and the two biggest sit on measured, mechanical causes (walls of text; invisible attributions; construction-banned synthesis) that this plan removes at the root. HONEST SHAPE OF THE PATH, stated without re-targeting: the first full pass will likely land 0.545-0.565 — BELOW target — because insight 0.58 and comprehensiveness 0.58 both exceed every value ever measured on this task; the wheel exists to close exactly that gap, one resolvable lever at a time, and the downside case (insight and compreh stalling at ~0.555) sums to ~0.557, a miss reported as a miss. The named closers for that case, in order of expected yield: (1) a real full-text channel (paid S2/OpenAlex key or institutional TDM) converting ~20 -> 50+ full-text cards, which feeds both methods texture and adjudication depth; (2) attribution-density iterations (110 -> 130+); (3) readability above the 0.52 herd value.
input.ceiling_honesty: THE ONE THRESHOLD IS 0.5578 + 0.0094. cellcog's task-72 row is unpublished (its scores/ dir ships only race_result.txt — verified); the regression band for its task-72 runs ~0.537-0.575, and this plan is built for the 0.575 BRANCH (design point 0.572, wheel continues past 0.5578 while levers still clear +0.0094), not the convenient 0.556 branch. WHERE IT CAN HONESTLY FALL SHORT: (a) insight 0.58 requires the judge to reward adjudication over assertion at band-jump magnitude — no system has measured above 0.5708 on this dimension; (b) comprehensiveness superiority rests on judge-visible attribution density and matrix breadth, not groundable-text superiority, because the measured funnel (journal_corpus_content.json: 36/70 groundable; 2024+ lane ~47% usable text) caps us at ~60-75 groundable cards — grounding parity with cellcog, not the 1.5x the last plan booked. If both stall at ~0.555 the total is ~0.557 and the report says MISSED, with those two numbers and the closers named above. THE ASSUMPTION WE ARE STILL MAKING AND HAVE NOT TESTED — stated plainly: that the GPT-5.5 judge PAYS for visible scholarship at the magnitude this arithmetic requires. Everything measured so far is about what SURVIVES the cleaner (tags 7/7, format-E years 10/12, headings 5/5) — feature VISIBILITY — not about what the judge pays for those features — feature VALUE. We have never composed one full report in this architecture and scored it k=5; the visibility-to-value conversion rate is the plan's load-bearing unknown, and wheel iteration 1 exists to measure it before anything else is tuned. Second-order untested: COCI coverage for the non-economics matrix cells (its index is Crossref-open-references only), and whether a 15-16k-word draft survives the strict gates without a deletion cascade shrinking it below the length target — gate survival at this scale has never been run.
input.kill_rule: THREE LAYERS, NONE NEGOTIABLE. (1) LEVER KILL: k=5 paired scoring only (SD 0.0074); a lever is kept iff its paired delta >= +0.0094; a lever that fails once is reverted immediately — no tweak-and-retry of a dead lever. (2) RUN KILL: if two consecutive wheel iterations gain < +0.0094 total while the mean sits below 0.5578, STOP and report MISSED with the per-dimension gaps and the named closers (full-text channel, attribution density, readability) — no renarration, no 'beat every measured score' consolation sentence; the ONLY permitted success statement is 'mean k=5 >= 0.5578 + 0.0094 with all integrity gates green'. (3) INTEGRITY KILL (any red blocks scoring and ships nothing): scripts/synthesis_contract.py --self-test must keep ATTACK #1 ('imports a mechanism no premise states') REJECTED — the CI canary that the mechanism-pool scoping was never widened; cleaner_survival_test.py on the actual draft must pass its four assertions; the unscoped-negative-existential regex must return zero; the attribution binding gate must return zero mismatches; and any single fabricated number, entity, attribution, or mechanism found in the line-by-line READ kills the draft outright regardless of its score — the verified-grounding moat is the product; a high score obtained by breaking it is a loss.
